Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Cash-strapped SA women’s hockey side: Meet the team!

The SA women’s hockey team competing at the Paris Olympics 2024 had to resort to crowdfunding in order to make their way to France.

The South Africanwomen’s hockey teamcompeting at theParis Olympics 2024had to resort tocrowdfundingin order to make their way to France, as reported byThe South African website.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section

Although they haven’t yet met their funding goal, the players have persevered with their preparations.

With their available fund, the team recently embarked on an Asian exposure tour, which culminated in India.

However, head coachGiles Bonnethas rued missed opportunities for further match practice due to the lack of finances.

“Funding has been our biggest challenge, we have had to withdraw from three events, which means about 30 test matches of experience have been lost to this group,” Bonnet toldThe Times of India.

“On top of that we have not been able to train in a high-performance environment as frequently as we would have liked.”

South Africa have selected16women (and two reserves) as well as a four-person strong management team for the Paris Olympics.

Taheera Augousti, Quanita Bobbs, Stephanie Botha, Dirkie Chamberlain, Erin Christie, Kayla de Waal, Paris-Gail Isaacs, Marie Antoinette Louw, Ongeziwe Mali, Ntsopa Mokoena, Edith Molikoe, Kristen Paton, Hannah Pearce, Celia Seerane, Anelle van Deventer, Onthatile Zulu

Reserves: Aphiwe Dimba, Kayla Swarts

Management:Lynn Abrahamse, Giles Bonnet, Marcelle Keet, Wayne Lombard

Currently ranked18thin the world, the South African women’s team faces a formidable challenge at the Paris Olympics.

The South African women’s side has been drawn in Group B, alongside Australia, Argentina, Great Britain, Spain and the United States.

Only the top four finishers in each of the two pools will progress to the quarter-finals.

Sunday, 28 July vs Australia – 12:45

Monday, 29 July vs Argentina – 17:30

Wednesday, 31 July vs Great Britain – 10:30

Thursday, 1 August vs Spain – 17:30

Saturday, 3 August vs United States – 13:15

At the most recent Olympics in Tokyo, the SA women’s hockey team lost all five of their pool matches and finished 12th and last in the standings overall.

Bonnet has set his team the target of finishing between fifth and eighth place this time around.

“Just to put things into perspective, the USA received $1 million for their programme to Paris,” Bonnet toldgsport.co.zain April.

“This is equal to almost R18.5 million. We could run a fully professional programme for four years on that value.”

The SA women’s hockey team has had the good fortune of receiving financial support via CEO:MCSA & Showmax at MultiChoice Group LtdMarc Juryover the past two years, while SuperSport Schools andKelvin Wattrecently helped the squad travel to China and India.

“There have also been hockey people whom have given up their time to source funding for the team and for this we are very grateful, it has kept our program going,” Bonnet acknowledged.

TheSouth African women’s hockey sidecontinues to dominate in Africa and is the only team from the continent heading to France.

In 10 Africa Cup of Nations and African Games tournaments since 1994, the women’s team has taken gold in all of them.

But it’s on the global stage where the side has struggled.

“It’s impossible to compete with other countries, the small details will always be missing,” Bonnet added.

“The talent exists in our country but once they finish school the gap widens into a chasm that’s not possible to cross anymore.”

“The strong tertiaries are not the high performance solution for international hockey. Unfortunately, this is the reality of our sport currently, but it’s not the end game.”
